items.title: Sabah claim used as political tactic in Philippines, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hjiji Noor said the Sabah claim is often turned into a polemic by certain parties in the Philippines, particularly as a political tactic during election seasons. He said that Sabah's status as part of the Malaysian Federation is final and has been recognised by the United Nations (UN), leaving no room for further dispute. But the people of Sabah have long decided to be part of Malaysia.

items.title: Heatwave dries up Sungai Pahang in Chenor, exposes part of riverbed
items.summary: MARAN: Peninsular Malaysia's longest river, Sungai Pahang, appears to be experiencing one of its worst dry spells in recent years, with water levels rapidly declining. Certain sections of the river in Kampung Pesagi, Chenor, near here have almost completely dried up, leaving behind a narrow water channel along the banks and exposing large stretches of the riverbed. "Now, the water has completely receded, leaving the riverbed exposed.

items.title: 40pct rise in construction costs may push up house prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A potential 40 per cent spike in construction costs, fuelled by prolonged conflict in the Middle East, is expected to drive up property prices as developers struggle to mitigate rising material costs. Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Shah Alam Faculty of Built Environment's senior lecturer Dr Muhammad Azwan Sulaiman said that when input costs rise, developers are faced with two choices, either absorb the costs, which would affect profit margins, or pass a portion of them on to buyers through higher selling prices. He said these cost increases also place significant pressure on developers in terms of overall operating costs, including logistics, labour and financing.

items.title: Hajiji: Sabah not up for debate, issue just a political tactic in Philippines
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 28 — Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the Sabah claim is often turned into a polemic by certain parties in the Philippines, particularly as a political tactic during election seasons. Hajiji stressed that Sabah’s status as part of the Malaysian Federation is final and has been recognised by the United Nations (UN), leaving no room for further dispute. But the people of Sabah have long decided to be part of Malaysia.

items.title: JPJ introduces new rules allowing rooftop, commercial spaces for driving training
items.summary: BALING, March 28 — The Road Transport Department Malaysia has introduced new guidelines for the establishment of Driving Institutes (IMs), including allowing training circuits to be built in high-rise buildings, warehouses and commercial premises. JPJ Director-General Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the guidelines were developed in line with the Motor Vehicles (Driving Schools) Rules 1992 and the Road Transport Act 1987, aimed at strengthening the regulation of the driving institute industry. Aedy Fadly said interested parties are welcome to submit applications at any time, including proposals to convert unused land, buildings or large warehouses into driving institutes.

items.title: Heatwave splits tourism fortunes in Penang as visitors adapt to extreme temperatures
items.summary: Beach and water sports operators said the scorching weather has discouraged outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours, although mornings and evenings remain busy. Jet ski operator Khairul Izdihar Ismail, 36, who has been in the business since 2015, said the past few days had been extremely hot, resulting in fewer customers during the day. Khairul said they are now mainly depending on domestic tourists for business.

items.title: Umno supreme council to discuss Hishammuddin, Khairy's applications on April 17
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Umno supreme council will discuss the applications of several former leaders to rejoin the party on April 17. This includes the applications of Sembrong member of parliament Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the discussion would be held based on the spirit of "Rumah Bangsa", but the decision on the applications would not necessarily be announced at the meeting. Among those who have applied are Hishammuddin, Shahril Sufian Hamdan, Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Sharif and Datuk Maulizan Bujang, whose memberships were suspended for six years.

items.title: Targeted diesel subsidy for East Malaysia in the works, says Armizan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The government is currently studying a framework for targeted diesel subsidies in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan before initiating further discussions with the respective state governments. He clarified that the government has never decided against implementing targeted diesel subsidies there, explaining that the rollout is being done in stages. Armizan noted that while targeted diesel subsidies began in the peninsula in 2024, the nationwide targeted RON95 petrol subsidy was implemented in 2025.

items.title: Lawyer seeks High Court review of jail sentence for couple over indecent act at cemetery
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A lawyer has filed an appeal on behalf of a couple who were sentenced to one year's jail by the magistrate's court here on Thursday after pleading guilty to jointly committing an act of gross indecency at a Chinese cemetery. Datuk Naran Singh filed the appeal at the High Court yesterday. On Thursday, the magistrate's court sentenced M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, to one year's jail after they admitted to the charge under Section 377D of the Penal Code.

items.title: Defence Ministry rolls out JanaVeteran to help veterans earn up to RM3,000 monthly
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 28 — The implementation of the JanaVeteran project is capable of empowering Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) veterans through the generation of sustainable income, thereby reducing their dependence on welfare assistance and ensuring the well-being of the veteran community. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the project was implemented by Boustead Plantations Berhad (BPlant) through ginger cultivation using the Halia KayTi brand, with a focus on building the capabilities of veterans so they can be self-sufficient and increase their income potential. “This effort is in line with the Defence Ministry's goal to ensure the well-being of veterans not only in terms of welfare but also through job opportunities and a dignified income," he said at the launching ceremony of the JanaVeteran project in Telok Sengat here today.

items.title: PKR: Nurul Izzah resignation rumours just ‘fake news’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) has dismissed as “fake news” swirling rumours that its deputy president, Nurul Izzah Anwar, has resigned from the post. “Fake news,” Fahmi said simply. Nurul Izzah, who is the daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, secured the deputy presidency in last year’s party elections.

items.title: Main Kota Kinabalu roads will close on Monday for TYT’s official birthday celebration
items.summary: Kota Kinabalu Deputy District Police Chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said the affected roads include Jalan Tun Fuad Stephen, Jalan Bandaran, Jalan Gaya and Jalan Padang. He said the main parade venue will be Padang Merdeka Kota Kinabalu and several surrounding roads will be closed. Road closures and vehicle screening will begin as early as 5am on March 30 at Jalan Padang, Dewan, Simpang Bukit Bendera and Bulatan Bunga Raya.

items.title: Are Hishammuddin, Khairy set to rejoin Umno? Party to discuss applications on April 17
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 28 — The applications of several former Umno leaders, including Sembrong Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy Jamaluddin, to rejoin the party will be discussed at the Umno Supreme Council meeting on April 17. Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the discussion would be held based on the spirit of “Rumah Bangsa”, but the decision on the applications would not necessarily be announced at the meeting. Among those who have applied are Hishammuddin, Shahril Sufian Hamdan, Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Sharif and Datuk Maulizan Bujang, whose memberships were suspended for six years.

items.title: Yeoh: Residents Representative Council has no right to check licences, talk to business owners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Residents Representative Council (MPP) has no right to check licences or talk to business owners if it received complaints, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh. Following a viral video showing two MPP members speaking to businesses and a subsequent police investigation into alleged extortion linked to the matter, Yeoh said all enforcement matters fall under the jurisdiction of Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). "They (MPP) coordinate issues and complaints.

items.title: Middle East conflict: Increase in MSME fuel costs may reach 50pct
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Fuel costs among micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in the country are at risk of soaring by up to 50 per cent of total operating costs if the conflict in Middle East continues. SME Corporation Malaysia chief executive officer Rizal Nainy said the increase could occur following the rise in global oil prices, which have now surpassed US$100 per barrel and are expected to continue to rise if the conflicts do not ease. "According to a survey by SME Corp, fuel costs currently only contribute around 6.4 per cent of the total operating costs of MSMEs.

items.title: Pair convicted of indecent act at cemetery seeks review of 12-month jail term
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 28 — A couple sentenced to 12 months’ imprisonment by the Magistrate’s Court here for committing lewd acts at a cemetery last Sunday has filed an appeal at the High Court, challenging their sentences. M. Jegathesan, 58, on Thursday pleaded guilty to the offence under Section 377D of the Penal Code. — Bernama

items.title: Perikatan will consider MIC’s silence as rejection of membership offer, says Takiyuddin
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 28 — Secretary-General Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said today if MIC does not provide an official response on joining Perikatan Nasional (PN), soon, its lack of response will be considered a rejection. Takiyuddin revealed that MIC had submitted an official application to join PN on Nov 15, 2025. The application was approved by PN on Dec 9, 2025, and a formal offer letter was sent to MIC on March 23.

items.title: Malaysia cannot unilaterally withdraw Malbatt from Lebanon, says Khaled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia cannot unilaterally withdraw its Malbatt 850-13 peacekeepers from Lebanon as the deployment is subject to the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mandate. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the mission is under the United Nations (UN), including command and any decision on troop withdrawal. Khaled also said that Malbatt personnel had encounters with the Israel Defence Forces (IDF), but the situation remained under control with no untoward incidents.

items.title: More CCTV around KLCC to crack down on photo touts, says minister
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said her office will hold further discussions with KLCC Property Holdings Bhd and the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to increase the number of CCTV cameras in areas that attract local and foreign tourists. Yeoh said she also wants police officers and DBKL enforcement officers to patrol more frequently to prevent the issue from continuing. I urge the police to take action," she said.

items.title: Seven Malaysian tankers awaiting 'safe window' to transit Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: REMBAU: Seven Malaysian-owned tankers are currently awaiting the signal to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened tensions in the Middle East. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the vessels include three owned by Petronas, two by MISC Bhd, and one each by Sapura Energy and Shapadu Corporation Sdn Bhd. "Iran has stated there is no issue in allowing our ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: PKR denies Nurul Izzah resignation rumours
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Claims that Nurul Izzah Anwar has resigned as PKR deputy president are "fake news". PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil dismissed the rumours when contacted by the New Straits Times. Nurul Izzah, the daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, was elected deputy president for the 2025-2028 term in last year's party polls.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: More CCTV at KLCC to crack down on ‘photo touts’
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said her office will hold further discussions with KLCC Property Holdings Berhad and the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to increase the number of CCTV cameras in areas that attract local and foreign tourists. Yeoh said she also wants police officers and DBKL enforcement officers to patrol more frequently to prevent the issue from continuing. I urge the police to take action,” she said.

items.title: Water at Bukit Kwong dam may last until June if dry spell persists
items.summary: Kelantan Drainage and Irrigation Department (DID) director Dr Siti Fairus Zakaria said projections indicated the dam's water level could drop to 12m within the next three months under prolonged dry conditions. She said based on past operational records, the Bukit Kwong dam has never reached a critical dry state, with its dead storage level recorded at 11m. Siti Fairus said monitoring data showed the dam's water level had decreased from 15.7m on March 1 to 15m as of March 27.

items.title: Bukit Kwong dam reserves may last only till June if dry spell persists
items.summary: Kelantan Drainage and Irrigation Department (DID) director Dr Siti Fairus Zakaria said projections indicated the dam's water level could drop to 12m within the next three months under prolonged dry conditions. She said based on past operational records, the Bukit Kwong dam has never reached a critical dry state, with its dead storage level recorded at 11m. Siti Fairus said monitoring data showed the dam's water level had decreased from 15.7m on March 1 to 15m as of March 27.

items.title: BPlant's JanaVeteran project to provide RM3,000 monthly income for veterans
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Boustead Plantations Bhd (BPlant) JanaVeteran project is set to empower armed forces veterans through involvement in modern agriculture focused on high-value cash crops, providing them with sustainable livelihoods and guaranteed incomes. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said under the initiative, veterans are offered plots within BPlant estates to cultivate crops such as ginger through fertigation methods, with each participant guaranteed a monthly income of RM3,000. Under the pilot project, Khaled said it covers 12 hectares with 15 participants; however, the ministry plans to expand it nationwide across Boustead's 42 estates, with at least 10 per cent of each estate converted to cash crops for veteran participation.

items.title: Community-led waste system transforms pollution practices in Semporna village
items.summary: SEMPORNA: A structured, community-based waste management system is beginning to transform long-standing marine pollution practices in a coastal village here. The initiative, under the Semporna Waste Management Programme led by Reef Check Malaysia with support from Etika Sdn Bhd and local authorities, was introduced following a door-to-door audit at Kampung Sri Aman water village here, which exposed critical gaps in waste handling. The preparatory phase also included the distribution of colour-coded waste bins, installation of communal collection points, and ongoing awareness efforts to introduce household waste segregation.

items.title: Fuel costs could hit 50pc of micro SME expenses as oil prices climb, says SME Corp
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 28 — Fuel costs among micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in the country are at risk of soaring by up to 50 per cent of total operating costs if the conflict in West Asia continues. SME Corporation Malaysia chief executive officer Rizal Nainy said the increase could occur following the rise in global oil prices, which have now surpassed US$100 (RM393) per barrel and are expected to continue to rise if the conflicts do not ease. “According to a survey by SME Corp, fuel costs currently only contribute around 6.4 per cent of the total operating costs of MSMEs.

items.title: Ayob Khan recounts high-risk 2013 Syria mission to locate IS recruit
items.summary: Ayob said that since 2012, 122 Malaysians had joined IS in Syria, with 53 killed, 26 returning home and 43 remaining in the country. He said that the E8 Counter-Terrorism operations team was mobilised, led by Inspector Rafael and Corporal Wisley, to establish contact with NGOs in Syria. "The team entered Syria on Nov 27.

items.title: Diplomatic efforts succeed: Seven Malaysian ships to depart strait of Hormuz, says minister
items.summary: REMBAU, March 28 — Seven Malaysian vessels currently stranded in the Strait of Hormuz are expected to depart the strategic waterway soon, following successful diplomatic engagements between Malaysia and Iran. According to The Star, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan confirmed that the Iranian government has agreed to allow the ships to transit through the strait. “I have spoken to my Iranian counterpart, and this was followed by a call by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian,” Mohamad Hasan told reporters after attending a Hari Raya open house in Rantau today.

items.title: Claims of unfairness over Sabah, Sarawak diesel subsidies inaccurate, says Armizan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali has dismissed claims that the federal government is being "unfair" by maintaining bulk diesel subsidies in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan. Armizan noted that while targeted diesel subsidies began in the peninsula in 2024, the nationwide targeted RON95 petrol subsidy was implemented in 2025. He said the Finance Ministry is currently studying the framework for targeted diesel subsidies in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan before further discussions with the respective state governments.

items.title: Labuan Corp invites investment proposals for eight prime assets, including Pulau Papan and former hotel site
items.summary: LABUAN, March 28 — Labuan Corporation (LC) has issued a request for proposal (RFP) to local and foreign investors for the leasing and development of eight of its assets, including the site of the recently demolished Hotel Labuan. LC chief executive officer Rithuan Ismail said the leasing offer forms, available via the local authority’s portal, are open from March 19 to May 19, 2026. “The leasing of these eight assets is intended for commercial purposes, including mixed commercial and tourism-related developments.

items.title: Bush fire spreads across 10 hectares in Mukah as hot, windy weather fuels blaze
items.summary: KUCHING, March 28 — A bush fire in Kampung Penasu, Daro, Mukah, has affected an area of about 10 hectares, the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said in a statement today. “As of noon, the fire had spread to 10 hectares. Meanwhile, a separate fire in Kuala Tatau-Serupai in Tatau, Bintulu, affected about 400 hectares, with around 100 hectares extinguished yesterday.

items.title: Israel army reports war's first missile attack from Yemen
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's military reported a first missile launch from Yemen since the Middle East war began on Feb 28, after Iran's Houthi allies threatened to join the fighting. A military statement said Israeli forces had "identified the launch of a missile from Yemen toward Israeli territory, aerial defence systems are operating to intercept the threat." Yemen's Houthi movement warned on Friday it would join the war if US-Israeli attacks continue to hit its ally Iran or if more countries join the conflict.

items.title: Former cop recalls narrow escape from 1971 Sarawak jungle ambush
items.summary: KOTA BARU: More than five decades have passed, but for retired policeman Abu Hassan Idris, the memory of a near-fatal ambush in the jungles of Sarawak remains as vivid as ever. We were deployed to Sarawak for Ops Ngayau," he said at his home in Kampung Bechah Resak. Abu Hassan said the incident occurred during a routine patrol near Rejang Park in Sibu at 10am.

items.title: ‘Don’t forget the invitation’: PM Anwar’s playful message to newly-engaged couple Syed Saddiq and Bella
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim congratulated Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and his fiancée, singer-actress Bella Astillah, on their engagement with a light-hearted message on social media. " Tahniah, kad jemputan jangan lupa " (Congratulations, don't forget the invitation card), Anwar wrote on X, responding to Syed Saddiq's engagement announcement. Tahniah, kad jemputan jangan lupa https://t.co/tUzHCUK7M1 — Anwar Ibrahim (@anwaribrahim) March 28, 2026

items.title: ‘Sabah position is final’: Foreign Ministry says Philippine senator's claim is not government stance
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 28 — Malaysia will send a diplomatic note to the Philippines as soon as possible to reject any renewed claims on Sabah, as recently raised by its senator, Robin Padilla. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Malaysia has consistently sent such diplomatic notes whenever statements regarding claims over Sabah are raised by any party. He said Sabah's position as part of the Federation of Malaysia is final, as the people of the state had decided to join Malaysia through a legitimate process that was verified by the United Nations (UN).

items.title: Takiyuddin shows proof of MIC's bid to join coalition
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has produced evidence to prove that MIC applied to join the coalition last year. He said PN deliberated on the request before officially accepting MIC as a member party in December. "MIC was among the founders of PN.

items.title: Police to wrap up rape probe involving assemblyman's son next week
items.summary: MARAN: Police expect to complete investigations by next week into the alleged rape of a 16-year-old girl involving the son of a Pahang opposition assemblyman. Maran police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said several people had been called in to give their statements. "The process is nearing completion, and we hope to conclude investigations next week," he said when contacted.

items.title: New opposition leader to be named from Pas before end of March
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) will take into account the views of its members of parliament in the appointment of a new opposition leader. PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the appointment would be made by the end of March, ahead of the next parliamentary session. "The opposition leader will be chosen from among Pas MPs.

items.title: Perhilitan told to disclose details of 'loan' and location of elephants [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) must disclose details regarding the "loan" of 19 elephants after denying allegations that the mammals were sold. Association for the Protection of the Natural Heritage of Malaysia (Peka) president Rajesh Nagarajan said the department, in denying the claims, failed to disclose basic and critical information, including the elephants' destination. He said elephants are classified as Totally Protected Wildlife under the Wildlife Conservation Act, granting them the highest level of protection against hunting, trapping, and trade.

items.title: Malaysia to send diplomatic note to Philippines over Sabah claim
items.summary: REMBAU: Malaysia will send a diplomatic note to the Philippines to reject and protest any attempts to revive claims over Sabah, following remarks by Philippine Senator Robin Padilla. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Wisma Putra would dispatch the note soon to reiterate Malaysia's firm stance. "We will send a diplomatic note to state our protest and rejection of those statements.

items.title: Riding out negative impact of Mideast conflict
items.summary: The impact is transmitted through higher oil prices, trade disruptions, financial market volatility, and rising living costs. However, rising costs and uncertainty pose a more pressing challenge. Higher oil prices are driving up transportation, production and import costs, particularly for food and industrial inputs.

items.title: SWCorp Pahang issues 238 notices for littering, 19 face court action
items.summary: SWCorp Pahang director Cairul Hisham Jalaluddin said 19 individuals have faced court action in Raub, Temerloh, and Kuantan. Today, 12 offenders underwent community service orders for periods ranging from one to four hours, starting at 9am. Cairul Hisham said SWCorp allows offenders to apply to serve their community service at locations near their homes to ease logistics and reduce travel costs.

items.title: Twice in a month: Lawyer caught driving Ford Mustang without road tax
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 27-year-old lawyer was caught driving a Ford Mustang without road tax or insurance coverage for the second time in a month yesterday. Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department director Hamidi Adam said the man was stopped in the city centre while on his way home. Last year, Transport Minister Anthony Loke revealed that several prominent figures , including politicians, businessmen and even recipients of the "Tan Sri" title, were among vehicle owners who had failed to pay their road tax, with arrears amounting to RM35.7 million.

items.title: Road closure in Maran for Panguni Uthiram festival
items.summary: KUANTAN: The main road in front of the Sri Maranthandavar Temple in Sungai Jerik, Maran, and its surrounding areas will be closed from March 30 to April 1 for the Panguni Uthiram festival. The Maran district police, in a Facebook post, said the closure will take effect from 4pm on March 30 until 2pm on April 1. Access to the main road remains available via Jalan Simpang Bangas, continuing to Jalan Utama and Bandar Jengka.

items.title: Housing Ministry eyes University of Cambridge tie-up on sustainable urban planning
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is seeking to establish a Malaysia-Cambridge urban platform to pursue Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) 2030 under the Madani economy framework. Its Minister, Nga Kor Ming, said the proposed collaboration with the University of Cambridge would strengthen strategic partnerships with leading global institutions in pursuit of sustainable urban development. Other priorities include advancing climate-resilient housing through transit-oriented development and nature-based solutions, enhancing land and planning coordination via a national urban observatory, and reviving abandoned housing projects.

items.title: Traffic builds on major highways as Hari Raya travellers head home
items.summary: A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority, when contacted by Bernama, said congestion is currently concentrated along key stretches of the North-South Expressway and within the Klang Valley, while traffic on the East Coast Expressway remains manageable. “Meanwhile, on the North-South Expressway (E2), traffic heading north is slow from Senai Utara to Kulai, while southbound lanes are congested from Putra Mahkota to Nilai Utara,” he said. On the North-South Expressway Central Link (ELITE), southbound traffic is moving slowly from Bandar Serenia to Nilai Utara.

items.title: Concessionaire clarifies that fatal rider accident occurred on NPE, not LDP
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 28 — Amanat Lebuhraya Rakyat Berhad (ALR), the concessionaire of the Damansara-Puchong Expresswa (LDP), has issued a clarification regarding a fatal accident involving a food delivery rider on Thursday, saying this did not occur on its expressway as previously reported. In a statement, ALR confirmed that the accident took place at KM 3.0 of the New Pantai Expressway (NPE), on the stretch heading from Bandar Sunway towards Bangsar. The clarification addresses earlier reports that cited Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Supt M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly as saying the LDP was the location of the incident.

items.title: Misusing subsidies a betrayal of the country, says KPDN as enforcement tightens
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 28 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has issued a stern warning that any attempt by individuals or syndicates to take advantage of the current global conflict will not be tolerated. Its Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said misappropriating national subsidies is a betrayal of the people and the country. At the same time, 2,400 KPDN enforcement officers nationwide are on high alert to carry out inspections and monitoring activities on the ground.

items.title: Three Melaka dams see levels drop due to heat, state assures supply until April
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 28 — Water levels at three dams in Melaka have dropped between 10 and 30 per cent due to hot weather and lack of rain recently. State Works, Infrastructure, Public Facilities and Transport Committee chairman Datuk Hameed Mytheen Kunju Basheer, however, said that the water level remains at a normal reading and, so far, the water supply is expected to be sufficient for the state until April. “Based on the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) report, high rainfall is expected in the interior of the west coast, covering water catchment areas in this state,” he told reporters after the Ayer Limau state assembly ‘Aidilfitri Melaka Sayang Rakyat’ event here last night.

items.title: Socso plan to cover cross-border workers commuting to Singapore in final stage, says HR minister
items.summary: Minister R. Ramanan said the initiative is part of broader efforts to strengthen the country’s social safety net. “In addition, we are also finalising a study to extend social protection to mothers during childbirth,” he said in a statement yesterday. Reflecting on his first 100 days in office, Ramanan said Kesuma has prioritised policies that deliver direct and long-term benefits to the people, particularly in social protection, skills development and worker welfare.

items.title: Level 2 heatwave hits Kedah, Perlis and Hulu Perak, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Five districts in Kedah, Hulu Perak in Perak and the whole of Perlis recorded a Level 2 heatwave, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). Meanwhile, several other areas in Kedah — Bandar Baharu, Kulim, Kuala Muda, Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu and Langkawi — are under Level 1 (alert) status. A Level 2 heatwave is declared when the daily maximum temperatures range from 37 to 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while Level 1 refers to daily temperatures between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius over the same period.

items.title: #BellaSyedYes: Syed Saddiq and Bella Astillah confirm engagement, after proposal on Mount Kinabalu (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — After months of playful denials, coy social media posts, and a trail of clues that kept fans on the edge of their seats, it appears that Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and actress-singer Bella Astillah have finally taken the next big step in their relationship. The speculation had reached its peak on March 27 when Syed Saddiq's brother, influencer Space Dollah (Syed Abdullah Syed Abdul Rahman), seemingly confirmed the engagement during a live-streaming session. Bella's response?

items.title: Police rescue 14, arrest seven in Melaka human trafficking raids
items.summary: MELAKA, March 28 — Police detained seven individuals for alleged involvement in human trafficking and rescued 14 others believed to be victims of forced labour here today. Dzulkhairi said that in the same operation, police also rescued 14 victims aged between 25 and 36, comprising seven men and six women from the Philippines, as well as one woman from Myanmar. He said initial investigations found that all the victims were believed to have been trafficked for forced labour exploitation in online scam activities.

items.title: Body of teenage boy found after suspected crocodile attack
items.summary: KUCHING, March 28 — The body of a teenage boy who was suspected to have been a victim of a crocodile attack while bathing with his family along Sungai Sarawak in Kampung Segubang, Batu Kawa, was found this afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Operations Centre said that the body of the boy, 15, was found by family members during a search and rescue operation at about 5.30 pm not far from where he went missing. “The body of the victim was found without his left and right arms,” the centre said, adding that the body has been handed over to the police for further action.

items.title: Health Ministry aims to ease HKL MRI congestion with increased outsourcing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has reintroduced the Hospital Services Outsourcing Programme (HSOP) at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) to ease congestion and reduce waiting times for patients requiring magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) services. In a statement yesterday, HKL said the HSOP reduced patient waiting times by 33 per cent in 2025, from 24 weeks to 16 weeks. “In 2025, HKL optimised 96.8 per cent of its allocated slots, using 365 out of 377.

items.title: Speeding trailer in Port Klang triggers viral chaos as container falls, creating traffic snarl
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 28 — Traffic was congested along Jalan Kem in Port Klang, near here, yesterday after a container fell off a trailer believed to have been speeding. South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about noon, did not result in any casualties or damage to other vehicles. “Initial investigations found that the trailer was believed to be speeding when passing through the location, causing the container to become detached from the trailer before falling onto the road.

items.title: Merbok murder victim couple laid to rest
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 28 — The remains of a married couple believed murdered in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were laid to rest at 9pm last night at the Masjid Al-Atiq Muslim cemetery here. Two Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) vehicles carrying the remains of Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and his wife Suraya Man, 53, arrived at the mosque at 7 pm, after being claimed by their family from Hospital Sultanah Bahiyah in Alor Setar. About 300 people, comprising relatives and villagers, attended the burial ceremony, which began after Maghrib prayers.

items.title: Three Thai nationals arrested for attempting to smuggle petrol, diesel into Thailand
items.summary: KANGAR, March 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) detained three Thai men who were attempting to smuggle petrol and diesel into Thailand using modified vehicle tanks on Jalan Kangar-Padang Besar, near the Padang Besar Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security Complex (ICQS) yesterday. Its Northern Brigade Commander, SAC Balveer Singh a/l Mahindar Singh said the police arrested the men, aged between 22 and 45, after inspecting three pickup trucks during ‘Op Taring Bravo 1’ at 9am. "As a result of the inspection, it was found that there was a quantity of liquid, suspected to be petrol and diesel, in the modified tanks of each vehicle, believed to be for smuggling petrol and diesel into Thailand," he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Wangsa Maju scandal: As video of alleged extortion goes viral PKR tells two implicated members to step down from posts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Two individuals from the Wangsa Maju Federal Territory Residents’ Representative Council (MPPWP) have been ordered to immediately vacate their positions following a viral video alleging they solicited protection money from a business premises. Federal Territories PKR Leadership Council (MPN) chairman Datuk Azman Abidin said the decision against R. Vijeyndran and G. Paarthiydasan was reached during a Federal Territories PKR Political Bureau meeting yesterday. “The Political Bureau has also directed the Federal Territories PKR Disciplinary Committee to conduct a thorough internal investigation and report its findings to the bureau in the near future,” he said in a statement yesterday.
